jokes.text: I'm intercepting some telepathic energy... that's telling me it's the eight of diamonds. That's amazingly close. Gee, I got it wrong.
jokes.setup: Gob claims to use telepathic powers to guess a card
jokes.punchline: He's wrong and acts like being wrong is still impressive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob delusion - treating failure as success. Strong character-specific comedy that establishes his incompetence while maintaining his confidence.

jokes.text: You know, it's a new trick, so I'm still kind of tweaking my nipples... The presentation was great.
jokes.setup: Gob means to say he's tweaking the trick
jokes.punchline: He accidentally says he's tweaking his nipples, George Michael politely ignores it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that creates awkwardness. George Michael's polite response amplifies the cringe. Economical and unexpected.

jokes.text: You might wanna button that shirt up. No, I'm good.
jokes.setup: Michael suggests Gob button his shirt
jokes.punchline: Gob refuses, implying he's deliberately showing skin
jokes.explanation: Sets up Gob's inappropriate spring break behavior. Visual gag that establishes character motivation.

jokes.text: Wow. He's amazing. He hasn't done it, and you're holding a four of spades in your hand.
jokes.setup: Fan praises Gob's magic
jokes.punchline: Michael points out Gob hasn't actually done the trick and got the card wrong
jokes.explanation: Michael as straight man pointing out absurdity. Good economy and structure.

jokes.text: I don't care. I'm loving the ride, you know?
jokes.setup: Fan admits Gob failed the trick
jokes.punchline: Fan doesn't care about the failure, enjoys the experience anyway
jokes.explanation: Satirizes how people enjoy spectacle regardless of competence. Less character-specific but good concept.

jokes.text: There's not gonna be any coeds. There certainly isn't gonna be any magic. It is a path to a lonely life where people mock you. And you don't even realize it.
jokes.setup: Michael warns George Michael about magic
jokes.punchline: The brutal honesty about Gob's life, with the kicker that Gob doesn't realize he's mocked
jokes.explanation: Devastating character insight delivered matter-of-factly. The escalation to 'you don't even realize it' is perfectly cruel.

jokes.text: Michael, I'm a magi... I see what you did.
jokes.setup: Gob starts to defend himself as a magician
jokes.punchline: He realizes Michael just proved his point about not realizing he's being mocked
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where Gob catches Michael's demonstration in real time. Perfect economy and immediate callback payoff.

jokes.text: We're not Orthodox.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asks if they stay open during spring break
jokes.punchline: Michael treats the business like a religion with denominations
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay treating business practices like religious observance. Very economical delivery.

jokes.text: Kitty, isn't that your phone? No, I'm on the phone with your dad. That one's your line.
jokes.setup: Phone ringing, Michael asks if it's Kitty's
jokes.punchline: Kitty reveals she's already on a phone and there's another line ringing
jokes.explanation: Establishes Kitty's chaos and confusion. Functional workplace comedy.

jokes.text: Kitty, these are not the files I asked for. Oh, then I don't know what I shredded.
jokes.setup: Michael complains about wrong files
jokes.punchline: Kitty casually reveals she may have destroyed important documents
jokes.explanation: Escalates from minor mistake to major disaster with Kitty's casual delivery. Good foreshadowing of document issues.

jokes.text: No, I have been Googling your father. So I've heard.
jokes.setup: Kitty explains what she's been doing instead of work
jokes.punchline: Michael's weary acknowledgment implies this is known inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo with good deadpan response. Sets up Kitty's obsession with George Sr.

jokes.text: She's been out for two weeks with this chest thing... but she's had no problems cashing the checks I've been sending her.
jokes.setup: Michael explains Kitty's absence for a chest problem
jokes.punchline: Implies the 'chest thing' is cosmetic surgery while she's still taking pay
jokes.explanation: Visual gag setup with good irony about work ethic. Foreshadows the breast enhancement reveal.

jokes.text: Buster got a perforated heart and Gob got that receding hairline... but you and I pretty much dodged the bullet.
jokes.setup: Lindsay talks about family genetics
jokes.punchline: Treats a receding hairline as equivalent to a serious medical condition
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and mismatched severity. Character-appropriate vanity from Lindsay.

jokes.setup: Negotiating a split after Michael suggests 60/40
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's math creates a 110% total, Michael agrees anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Lindsay's mathematical incompetence and Michael's distracted agreement. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: I sold you for a pack of cigarettes.
jokes.setup: Tobias is in prison for research
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of being sold, treating it as normal prison experience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy with perfect economy. Tobias's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: There's only one man I've ever called 'coward'. That's Brian Doyle-Murray.
jokes.setup: White Power Bill asks if Tobias thinks he's a coward
jokes.punchline: Tobias has only called one person coward - a random character actor
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random and specific. The specificity of Brian Doyle-Murray makes it absurd and memorable.

jokes.text: What I'm calling you... is a television actor. Ouch.
jokes.setup: Tobias delivers what he thinks is the ultimate insult
jokes.punchline: He calls him a television actor, which is exactly what Tobias is
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke about Tobias's actor pretensions. Self-aware without breaking character. The 'Ouch' reaction sells it perfectly.

jokes.text: A fact that took Lindsay and Maeby an hour and a half to ascertain.
jokes.setup: They visit who they think is Nana
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals it took 90 minutes to realize it wasn't her
jokes.explanation: Classic AD narrator deadpan. The specific time detail makes their obliviousness funnier.

jokes.text: Go! Go! It's not her! Drop the photo album! We're not in the photo album! Drop the pie!
jokes.setup: They realize their mistake
jokes.punchline: Panicked escape with increasingly specific items to abandon
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy escalation. The photo album detail is particularly funny.

jokes.text: Much better. Man, I was really stuffed up there! Up here, Michael. Up here.
jokes.setup: Kitty talks about feeling better from being sick
jokes.punchline: She's clearly showing her breasts while Michael tries not to look
jokes.explanation: Sets up the breast enhancement revelation. Michael's discomfort is well-played.

jokes.text: having a margarita made in my mouth
jokes.setup: Kitty describes her spring break plans
jokes.punchline: Awkward phrasing that sounds sexual
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo that's character-appropriate for Kitty's inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: I work for your father. You don't have the hiring and firing power. I absolutely do, and you're fired.
jokes.setup: Kitty claims Michael can't fire her
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately proves her wrong by doing exactly that
jokes.explanation: Good power dynamic comedy. Sets up ongoing conflict.

jokes.text: But take a good look, 'cause it's the last time!
jokes.setup: Kitty threatens Michael before leaving
jokes.punchline: She flashes him again while delivering the threat
jokes.explanation: Establishes Kitty's signature move that becomes a running gag. Character-specific inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: That's the seventh nipple I've seen today.
jokes.setup: Michael reacts to Kitty's flashing
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact count implies this is a regular occurrence
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery. The specific count and casual tone make it funnier.

jokes.text: You don't fire crazy. You never fire crazy.
jokes.setup: George Sr. learns Michael fired Kitty
jokes.punchline: Treating crazy as a protected class with its own HR rules
jokes.explanation: Brilliant workplace wisdom delivered with authority. Highly quotable and universally applicable.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, you paid for those, didn't you?
jokes.setup: Michael realizes what Kitty's 'chest thing' was
jokes.punchline: He realizes the company paid for her breast enhancement
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier setup about the chest thing and sick leave.

jokes.text: Well, he's my twin brother. I can show you. It was funny.
jokes.setup: George Sr. talks about firing his twin brother
jokes.punchline: Offers to demonstrate the facial expression since they're twins
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic combined with dark family cruelty. The twin angle makes it uniquely weird.

jokes.text: Oh, God, there's nothing to hold on to down there. You're like a boy.
jokes.setup: Physical interaction between father and son
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate comment about Michael's body
jokes.explanation: Awkward physical comedy that relies on inappropriate touching. Less clever than other jokes.

jokes.text: Tobias had even produced a videotape... but its brief success was due to its misleading name.
jokes.setup: Narrator mentions Tobias made a video
jokes.punchline: Implies people bought it thinking it was something else
jokes.explanation: Good callback that implies the video was mistaken for something sexual. Well-constructed reveal.

jokes.text: You've got a little pocket there, Annyong. Annyong. That's not getting old.
jokes.setup: Lindsay tries to use Annyong's purse
jokes.punchline: Annyong says his name, Michael sarcastically notes the repetition
jokes.explanation: Running gag acknowledgment. Michael's sarcasm about it not getting old is self-aware.

jokes.text: In fact, Lucille's mother had been dead for six months.
jokes.setup: Lucille talks about mother's wonderful cruise activities
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals the mother is dead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy reveal that recontextualizes everything. Perfect timing and structure.

jokes.text: She gained 10 pounds... there's so much food on that boat. She's up to 74!
jokes.setup: Lucille describes Nana's cruise experience
jokes.punchline: Reveals 92-year-old grandmother weighs only 74 pounds
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about elderly frailty while Lucille maintains elaborate lie

jokes.text: Ninety-two years old, and she never tried pesto.
jokes.setup: Lucille continues describing dead mother's 'activities'
jokes.punchline: Absurd detail about pesto given the context
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny detail that shows Lucille's elaborate deception. The specificity makes it absurd.

jokes.text: If you'll be so kind as to expose your breast, please.
jokes.setup: Gob performing magic for George Michael and woman
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately formal request for woman to expose herself
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate inappropriate behavior. The formal phrasing makes it more awkward.

jokes.text: Don't worry. He's gonna be covered with a blue dot. He approached me.
jokes.setup: Michael finds George Michael in compromising situation
jokes.punchline: Gob treats it like a legitimate TV production with censorship
jokes.explanation: Absurd justification that treats exploitation video like legitimate media. Good callback to earlier setup.

jokes.text: Wow, you get really girlie after, huh?
jokes.setup: Gob expresses emotional need for love
jokes.punchline: Michael's blunt observation about Gob's post-coital vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Good character observation. Michael's matter-of-fact delivery of uncomfortable truth.

jokes.text: The Jews, I guess. Sure. But I think you need to look deeper.
jokes.setup: Tobias asks where Bill's hate comes from
jokes.punchline: Tobias treats obvious racism as surface-level therapy issue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy showing Tobias's therapeutic obliviousness to obvious hatred. Dark but character-perfect.

jokes.text: I hate you! You hate White Power Bill. I hate the government! You hate White Power Bill. I hate my father!
jokes.setup: Tobias tries therapy technique
jokes.punchline: Regardless of what Bill says, Tobias redirects it to hating himself
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and repetition. Shows Tobias's therapeutic determination despite obvious failure.

jokes.text: Like Dorothy. The Wicked Witch is dead. All hail Dorothy!
jokes.setup: White Power Bill is beaten
jokes.punchline: Prisoners treat Tobias like Dorothy from Wizard of Oz
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier setup about being a Friend of Dorothy. Absurd but earned.

jokes.text: Who are you asking back, Michael? Me? Or these?
jokes.setup: Michael asks Kitty to return to work
jokes.punchline: Kitty gestures to her breasts as separate entities
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate behavior that treats her breasts as separate entities she can negotiate with.

jokes.text: I've gotta get out of here. I'm a part of the story. I can't be a part of the story.
jokes.setup: Situation escalates around Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael's awareness he's becoming part of the mess he tries to avoid
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment about Michael's role as the stable one. Shows character self-awareness.

jokes.text: Say good-bye to these. 'Cause it's the last time.
jokes.setup: Kitty's final threat to Michael
jokes.punchline: She flashes him again with her signature line
jokes.explanation: Solidifies the running gag with perfect character consistency. The repetition becomes the joke.

jokes.text: Talk you off? Talk you off what, Pop-Pop? When's that voice gonna drop?
jokes.setup: George Sr. on phone with George Michael
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s inappropriate interpretation and comment about voice
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate inappropriate comment from George Sr. The voice comment adds insult.

jokes.text: If I clean it up, it's not really a sentence.
jokes.setup: Asked what George Sr. said
jokes.punchline: George Michael's innocent way of saying it was profane
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing George Michael's innocence and precision with language.

jokes.text: Call me the Tin Man. I'm a Friend of Dorothy's now.
jokes.setup: Tobias has painted shoes red
jokes.punchline: References both Wizard of Oz and prison gay terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever dual reference that works on multiple levels. Good payoff to earlier setup.

jokes.text: But if he makes it disappear, won't everyone see me standing there? Let's just sit quietly and consider how ridiculous that statement was.
jokes.setup: George Michael worries about being seen if he sneaks on the yacht
jokes.punchline: Maeby points out the obvious logical flaw
jokes.explanation: Good logic joke with perfect deadpan response from Maeby. The pause instruction makes it funnier.

jokes.text: None of your damn business. Get the hell out of here. You don't have the magic in you! You never did!
jokes.setup: George Michael comes to help with magic
jokes.punchline: Gob's sudden hostile rejection to protect his deal with Michael
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gob forced to hurt someone he cares about. The magic line is particularly cruel.

jokes.text: I bought you. Remember to let it steep for five minutes.
jokes.setup: George Sr. reveals he bought Tobias
jokes.punchline: Immediately transitions to polite tea instructions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant tonal whiplash from dark revelation to domestic politeness. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: So Frightened Inmate #2 isn't frightened at all. He's angry. He's a crabby old coot... and he needs love... just like everyone.
jokes.setup: Tobias analyzes George Sr.'s emotional state
jokes.punchline: Therapeutic breakthrough delivered in actor terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment combining therapy and acting. Good callback to his character research.

jokes.text: I sunk it! I sunk the yacht! At least I think I sunk it. I mean, I blew it up, and I don't see it anywhere.
jokes.setup: Gob reveals how he made the yacht disappear
jokes.punchline: Admission of actual destruction and uncertainty about the result
jokes.explanation: Great reveal that subverts magic expectations. The uncertainty about success makes it funnier.

jokes.text: $900,000 worth of insurance on it. And got rid of the evidence. How about a 'good job'?
jokes.setup: Gob explains the insurance value
jokes.punchline: Treats insurance fraud as an accomplishment worthy of praise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment wanting approval for criminal activity. Good callback to his need for validation.

jokes.text: Nana was on that yacht! What? You killed Nana! She's lying! She's been dead for six months. For about six months.
jokes.setup: Lucille claims Nana was on the yacht
jokes.punchline: Revelation that she's been dead, with Lindsay's correction of timeframe
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the dead grandmother plot. The timing correction from Lindsay is perfect.

jokes.text: You can't have it. It's in a trust fund for Annyong. Annyong.
jokes.setup: Lucille reveals money is in trust for Annyong
jokes.punchline: Annyong responds with his name as always
jokes.explanation: Running gag continuation with plot relevance. Annyong's consistency is the joke.

jokes.text: Bluth Company. George Michael speaking, not Kitty.
jokes.setup: George Michael answers the phone
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he's not Kitty, referencing her absence
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing George Michael's awareness of office dynamics. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I don't need a real spring break. I really think I am much better off here alone in this office.
jokes.setup: George Michael talks about spring break
jokes.punchline: His sad acceptance of isolation as preferable
jokes.explanation: Poignant character moment that's both sad and funny. Shows the cost of Michael's overprotection.

jokes.text: She showed her knobs in the steakhouse, sir.
jokes.setup: Officer explains why people remember Kitty
jokes.punchline: Formal police terminology for indecent exposure
jokes.explanation: Excellent euphemism delivered with police formality. Great callback to earlier scene.
